zh.Thornton, Cheshire, , England: 英國英格蘭爾桑頓 馬福
zh.Marston Trussell, Northamptonshire, Engl: 英國諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 特波瑟爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Hawise de Trussell於1270年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 特波瑟爾時，她的父親Sir William de Trussell I45歲，母親Roesia Pantulf43歲。她於1293年與William Hellesby在英國英格蘭爾桑頓 馬福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1332年在英國爾享年62歲。

zh-hans.Thornton, Cheshire, , England: 英国英格兰爾桑頓 馬福
zh-hans.Marston Trussell, Northamptonshire, Engl: 英国諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 特波瑟爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Hawise de Trussell于1270年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 特波瑟爾时，她的父亲Sir William de Trussell I45岁，母亲Roesia Pantulf43岁。她于1293年与William Hellesby在英国英格兰爾桑頓 馬福结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1332年在英国爾享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Trussell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English trus(s)el(le) ‘bundle, parcel’ (Old French troussel), perhaps for a porter (see Truss ), or from the same word in the sense ‘stamp, mould (for stamping coins)’, for someone who minted coins.
variant of Thrussell, a nickname from Middle English thros(t)el, thrus(t)el ‘song thrush’ (Old English thros(t)le). Compare Thrush .

variants.name: Truss
meanings: English (mainly Essex and Middlesex): metonymic occupational name from Middle English trusse ‘bundle, package’ (Old French trousse), perhaps for a porter. See also Trussell .
Americanized form of Ukrainian Trus: nickname from trus ‘rabbit’, typically applied to someone thought to be a coward.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
meanings: This female name (which has been corrupted down to Avice and Avis) is from Hadewisa, formed from the German name Hedvig, same as Edwig, which Wachter translates 'propugnator felicitatis;' say, 'fortunate soldier.'

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Hawise de Trussell was born in 1270, in Marston Trussel, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir William de Trussell I, was 45 and her mother, Roesia Pantulf, was 43. She married William Hellesby in 1293, in Thornton Mayow, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died in 1332, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Hawise de Trussell est née en 1270, à Marston Trussel, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Trussell I, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Roesia Pantulf, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé William Hellesby en 1293, à Thornton Mayow, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1332, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Hawise de Trussellが1270年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアマーストン トルセルで生まれた時，父Sir William de Trussell Iは45歳，母Roesia Pantulfは43歳であった。1293年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアソルントン メイオウでWilliam Hellesbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1332年にイギリスチェシュアイアで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Hawise de Trussell(이)가 1270년 영국 노르섬프토실르 마스턴 트루셀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Trussell I(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Roesia Pantulf(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1293년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 토른턴 메이요에서 William Hellesby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1332년 영국 체실르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Hawise de Trussell родилась в 1270 г. в местности марстон трассел, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Trussell I было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Roesia Pantulf, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hellesby в 1293 г. в местности таурнтон меиоу, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1332 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.
